what_it_does: Defines the Settings canvas — system-wide configuration focused on the OpenAI image-generation surface. It lets the user enter/save/test OpenAI credentials (API key, org id, optional proxy), pull and pick from a live list of image models grouped by tier, set capability-aware image defaults (size/quality/style/n), toggle Vi image-gen and safe mode, and view/clear local browser storage. All state is read from and written to window.cvOpenAI / window.cvImageStore / window.cvStudioStorage.
observations: Reads settings via window.cvOpenAI.getSettings() and subscribes to cvOpenAI (and subscribeModels) for live updates.
On mount, auto-fetches the model list only if configured and no API-sourced cache exists (avoids needless fetch).
Credentials are kept in pending* state and committed on Save via cvOpenAI.updateSettings; key is masked (first 6 + bullets + last 4) with a reveal toggle.
Test connection calls cvOpenAI.testConnection() and reports model counts; the test refreshes the model cache as a side effect.
Connection-status banner switches copy based on cvOpenAI.isConfigured(), explaining that unconfigured = Imagery AI Studio runs in 'preview mode'.
ModelPicker groups models into flagship/standard/legacy tiers and renders capability badges (generate/edit/variations/style/n×maxN), sizes, and quality-tier counts per card.
ModelDefaults is capability-aware: size/quality/style/n options adapt to cvOpenAI.getModelCapabilities(selectedModel); unsupported current size is shown as '(not supported)'.
Storage section shows MB used against an ~5MB browser quota meter and image count, with a confirm-gated 'Clear all studio state'.
formatRelative renders human 'just now / Nm / Nh / Nd ago' timestamps.
Privacy copy: 'Stored locally in your browser. Never sent anywhere except OpenAI.'
standouts: Capability-driven UI: the model list is pulled live ('new models appear automatically as they ship') and the defaults form reshapes to exactly what the selected model supports.
Optional proxy URL to route around networks that block direct browser→OpenAI calls.
Local-first, privacy-forward framing — credentials and all studio state live in the browser, with an explicit storage meter and one-click wipe.
Connection state gates downstream features (Imagery AI Studio preview mode, Vi image awareness).
conventions: Follows window-global registration and React-via-globals; uses dsa-* and cv-st-* class names with CSS-var styling.
Subscribe-driven re-render keeps the UI a projection of cvOpenAI state.

Notably routes image config directly through window.cvOpenAI rather than through CV_AI's resolveProvider('openai-image') — the project CLAUDE.md says image work should go through CV_AI; this settings surface manages the underlying cvOpenAI store directly.
concerns: Direct coupling to window.cvOpenAI for all credential/model logic sits beside the project's directive to route model work through CV_AI; this is the config layer for that provider, but it bypasses the CV_AI abstraction.
saveCredentials trims and stores the raw API key in browser storage (necessary for client-side calls, but a plaintext-key-in-localStorage exposure).
Hardcoded ~5MB browser quota assumption for the meter; actual quota varies by browser.
Several capabilities depend on optional-chained methods (getCachedModels?., subscribeModels?., cvImageStore?.) that silently no-op if absent, so a missing store degrades quietly.
modelError surfaces fetch failures but the auto-fetch-on-mount path swallows nothing visibly until a manual refresh.
notes: The OpenAI image-provider control panel: enter/test credentials, pick a live-fetched model by tier, set capability-aware defaults, toggle Vi image-gen/safe-mode, and manage local storage — all driven off window.cvOpenAI.
questions: Why is image config managed via window.cvOpenAI directly rather than through CV_AI.resolveProvider('openai-image') as the project directive suggests?
Where do safeMode and enableForVi actually take effect in the generation path?
Is the ~5MB quota assumption accurate across the target browsers, and what happens on quota overflow?
purpose_vs_actual: Match — the header ('Configure the AI services your studio uses... Vi connects to OpenAI for image generation') matches the implemented credentials/model/defaults/storage configuration surface.
apparent_themes: Connection state as a gate for downstream AI features
intention_signals: Aims to make connecting and tuning the OpenAI image provider effortless and self-explaining — pulling models live so the UI never goes stale, adapting options to each model's real capabilities, and keeping credentials/state local with a visible storage budget.
novelty: The live, tier-grouped model picker with per-model capability badges and a defaults form that reshapes to the selected model's actual supports — configuration that tracks the provider's evolving catalogue automatically.
contribution: Contributes the image-provider settings canvas: credential entry/test/mask, live tiered model picker with capability badges, capability-aware image defaults, Vi/safe-mode toggles, and a local-storage meter with wipe, all bound to window.cvOpenAI.
summary_for_embedding: A React Settings canvas that configures the OpenAI image provider via window.cvOpenAI: save/test masked credentials and proxy, a live tier-grouped model picker with capability badges, capability-aware defaults, Vi/safe-mode toggles, and a local-storage meter with clear.
intention_for_embedding: To let the user connect and tune the OpenAI image-generation provider with a self-updating, capability-aware UI while keeping credentials and studio state local to the browser.
